clientMessages.author: max.hastings
clientMessages.content:
Hey Kenneth, good question. Nothing has been changed on your Facebook page on our end.

The reason this doesn’t affect what we’re doing is because Meta ads don’t rely on people searching for your business. Instead, the ads are shown directly in the Facebook and Instagram feeds of homeowners in your target area who fit the profile of someone likely to need remodeling.

So people typically see the ad first, click on it, and then submit their info from there. They’re not usually going to your page by searching your business name like they would with Google.

Everything on the campaign side is still set up correctly and ready to be launched as planned 👍

clientMessages.author: davi_dinicio
clientMessages.content:
Don't worry if people have a hard time finding you by typing your name in the Facebook search bar. Our ads work differently, they are pushed directly onto the screens of targeted homeowners in your area. The customer doesn't need to search for you; the ad will automatically appear in front of them.

That small charge you saw was Meta (Facebook) doing a temporary test charge to verify your card before running the ads. Because your bank blocked it as fraud, Facebook is currently pausing the account. Please call your bank to authorize charges from Facebook/Meta. Once they approve it, we can launch.
